title: Implement and test Raft leader election
description: Bring up Raft terms, election timeouts, and vote rules so the cluster elects exactly one leader per term and recovers after partitions.
body: Steps: 1. Give each node a current term, randomized election timeout, and persistent voted-for record. 2. On timeout, increment term, become candidate, and request votes carrying last-log index/term. 3. Grant a vote only once per term and only if the candidate's log is at least as up-to-date. 4. Require a majority to win; step down on seeing a higher term. 5. Have the leader send periodic heartbeats to suppress new elections. Rollback point: before changing the term-persistence format. Validation: a partition test elects exactly one leader per term and converges to a single leader after heal; a split vote retries without two leaders. Inspect logs for any term with two leaders, which is a safety violation.
